name: Warden
canAlways: Stand up for those you care for.
Keep both eyes on someone.
Stamp your foot and get everyone’s attention.
Perform a display of strength.
Recount a tale of battles old.
Ask: “What aren’t you telling me?”
Offer to teach someone about something you have no expertise in. If they take the offer, they get a Luck Point.
description: Wardens are fearsome warlords and unshakeable vanguards. The sword that cuts down all foes; the shield unyielding in danger.

startingWeapons.heading: Weapons (2)
values:
Oathsworn Blade: d10, Hefty
& Battle-Hardened Shield: 10^^gd^^, 4^^wd^^
Champion’s Greataxe: d12, Two-handed, can strike an adjacent target once each turn
Iron-borne Hatchets (2): d6, Light (d4), Thrown (30 ft).
Cavalier’s War-Pike: d10, Two-handed, Reach

Banner-Spear: d10, Versatile (d8), Hefty, deals full damage while using Tower-Shield
& Tower Shield: 14gd, 6wd, Slow
Stygian Greathammer: 2d6, two-handed. If you break a target’s gd, knock them **Prone**.
Beast Claws: 2d4, one per hand, causes Unguarded targets to **Bleed**.

Gilded Blade: d10, Versatile (d8), priceless but you cannot part from it
& Parrying Dagger: d6, Light (d4), +5gd while offhanding

startingArmor.heading: Armor (1)
values: Battle-wrought plate, passed down generations. (mail, grieves, helm, A3)
War-priest’s vestments. Vibrant robes bolstered by chain (mail, A1)
Dawnweave accompaniments (boots and gloves) (grieves, gloves, A2)
The husk of long decommissioned golem (mail, helm, A2)

startingBelongings.heading: Belongings (2)
values: Divine crest. Glows brightly in the presence of fiends.
Crimson locket. Smells faintly of roses.
A hooded lantern that needs no fuel. Its flame smells of roasted marshmallows.
A small vessel of ceremonial war ink.
Hexsteel stake. Once plunged into a surface can only be removed by you.
Animal pelt cape. Keeps you warm through frigid winters.

name: Skylark
canAlways: Have something you’re not supposed to.
Find a way out from between a rock and a hard place.
Spill the beans.
Tell which way is north.
Speak the language of thieves.
Keep calm and focus.
Exclaim: “Do I have the deal for you!” If they hear you out, they get a Luck Point."
description: Skylarks are enduring wayfarers and elegant slayers. The dagger that strikes unseen; the arrow that guides armies.

startingWeapons.heading: Weapons (2)
values: Crescent Blade: d10, Hefty, +1d8 under the light of the moon, cannot use with a shield.
Hunting Bow: d10, Two-handed, Ranged (120 ft.)
Twin Fang Daggers (2): d6, Light (d4), Thrown 30 ft.
Shroudtouched Crossbow: d12 Two-handed, Heavy, Ranged (100 ft.), Slow
Duelist’s Rapier: d8, Hefty, +6gd & +6wd if wielding no other weapons.
Sharpeye’s Handcannon: d6, Light (d4), Ranged (30ft.) can be used in melee.
Betreyal’s Sting: d6, Light (d4), +2d8 to targets who trust you.
Silver Sword: 2d6, Two-handed, priceless but you cannot part from it

startingArmor.heading: Armor (1)
values: Clandestine leather. (mail, grieves, A2)
Rover’s camoflage, worn from years of use. (mail, gloves, A2)
Ō-yoroi, bestowed by your master.  (helm, mail, A2)
Moon-blessed vestments (mail, A1)

startingBelongings.heading: Belongings (2)
values: Feline grace talisman. While wearing, you take no fall damage.
Satchel of wolfsbane. Has 3 doses, but you know how to get more.
A pair of loaded dice.
A weathered scroll, containing forbidden secrets. Indecipherable.
Mystery key. 5% chance to unlock any given door, promptly breaks after opening it.
A whistle that only one species of animal can hear (you choose).

name: Magus
canAlways: Perform a minor miracle.
Jot down something important.
Put your faith in those around you.
Do something out of the ordinairy.
Cite your sources, hoping the help your case.
Recall the teachings of your master.
Ask: “Could you explain?” If they do, they get a Luck Point."
description: Magi are adept casters and revered scholars. Soothing waves to burnished skin; searing flames to foes worn thin.

startingWeapons.heading: Weapons (2)
values: Pair of Dueling Wands: d6, Light (d4), Elemental, Ranged (90 ft.)
Treewarden Staff: d10, Two-handed, Elemental, can be used as Ranged (60 ft.).

Sun/Moon-Touched Mace: d8, Hefty
& Divine Shield: 8gd, 6wd
Lightkeeper’s Rod: d6, Light (d4), Elemental, Ranged (90ft.), +d6 against those who walk in death.
Dragon-blessed Seal: +1 Fire/Water damage and +6wd while in offhand.
Spellspear: d6, Hefty, +d6 when used the same turn as a magic skill
Serpent Scepter: d10, Versatile (d8), Hefty, +d6 to creatures with scales.
Sword of Night/Day: d10, Versatile (d8), Hefty, +d6 Water/Fire to Wet/Burning targets

startingArmor.heading: Armor (1)
values: Scholar’s duds, from your academy days. (mail, A1)
Holy robes, sacred to your cloister. (mail, A1)
Dragon-scale vestures, origins known only to you. (mail, gloves, A2)
Apprentice robes, as recommended by your master. (mail, A1)

startingBelongings.heading: Belongings (2)
values: Relic of Sol/Luna. Speaking its command word causes it to glow brightly.
Fundementalist spellbook. You can replace one magic skill by studying for 8 hours.
A message your master requested you deliver, a reward bestowed on delivery.
Astral atlas. Referring to it allows you to easily navigate through the night.
A rare book on a niche subject that is special to you.
A necklace, given to you by your master in their dying breath. Describe its appearance.
